title: Checkpoints, Write-Ahead State, Crash Consistency, and Resume · Contract Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for checkpoints, write-ahead state, crash consistency, and resume and accepts only compiler plus runtime changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named network, DNS, browser, accessibility, security, load, legal-review, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for versioned crash-consistent crawl checkpoint. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case p…
objectives: Define which policy, seed, frontier, attempt, digest, report, and output states must survive and which can be recomputed.
Write bounded versioned state to a temporary file, sync by declared durability, validate, rename atomically, and retain recovery copies.
Return claimed or fetching items to an explicit retry state using attempt and idempotency rules after a crash.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Checkpoint Boundary · changed case · Quiz Checkpoint Bounda…
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript team handles case 6158 during an origin-fairness and event-loop capacity rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove state boundaries, atomic replacement, in-flight recovery, policy-version admission, and resume reconciliation; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Resume Reconciliation
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Resume Reconciliation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz TypeScript team handles case 1633 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove state boundaries, atomic replacement, in-flight recovery, policy-version admission, and resume reconciliation; URL, robots, body, parser, frontier, privacy, accessibility, and report budgets stay explicit; then reorder one frontier, p-limit, Promise, AbortSignal, stream, retry, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove no lost terminal records, duplicate external effects, missing frontier items, or unexplained count changes across interruption.

steps.why: Retrieving Resume Reconciliation through the concrete versioned crash-consistent crawl checkpoint case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prove no lost terminal records, duplicate external effects, missing frontier items, or unexplained count changes across interruption.
